steps.title: Identify the specific task
steps.content: The student wants to specify the number of gray wolves in Yellowstone. The notes state: "the number of gray wolves in the park has stabilized at roughly 100." The correct answer must include an actual population figure.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Gray wolves were reintroduced into Yellowstone, a national park in the northwest United States, in 1995.': States when wolves were reintroduced but gives no population number. ✗
  • Choice B — 'As of 1995, there were gray wolves living in Yellowstone, a national park in the northwest United States.': Mentions wolves existing as of 1995 but omits the current count. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The carrying capacity of an environment, such as Yellowstone, describes the maximum number of species that the environment can sustain over time.': Defines carrying capacity but provides no wolf population number. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Yellowstone is a national park that has roughly 100 gray wolves living in it.': Directly states the population figure from the notes — "roughly 100" — satisfying the goal of specifying the number. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
